concerns.text: T013 instructs downloading data from a generic NAB URL ('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/numenta/NAB...') instead of the specific CHERRL repository mandated by spec.md and plan.md. The task lacks the concrete, verifiable URL required to execute the download, making the task impossible to perform as written.

T032 references a baseline of 'last [deferred] of training steps'. The placeholder '[deferred]' is not a concrete value or a defined configuration parameter. An implementer cannot execute a grid search or statistical test without a specific integer or percentage value for the baseline window size.

T030 describes checking correlation but fails to specify the concrete 'halt' behavior required by spec.md FR-006 and plan.md Constitution Check VI. The task must explicitly state: 'If r > 0.8, raise a RuntimeError and exit with code 1' to be executable and verifiable.

T023 conflates the output of the detection phase (hacked_label) with the ingestion phase output file. While not strictly unexecutable, this violates the separation of concerns defined in the spec (FR-001 vs FR-003) and may confuse the implementer regarding which module produces which artifact. Refine to target the detector output file specifically.

T032 mandates a 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test' which contradicts spec.md FR-005 that explicitly requires a 'paired t-test'. While the plan.md mentions Wilcoxon as a complexity tracking note, the task must align with the functional requirement (FR-005) or explicitly justify the deviation as a methodological override. As written, it creates ambiguity on which statistical test to implement.
